model_narrative: We plan how to mine a group of open pits over a sequence of time periods. Each pit is divided into segments stacked from top to bottom, and the segments of a pit have to be taken in order from the top down. In every period we decide which segments are available to extract, how far down the extraction has progressed in each pit, how much material to take from each segment, and the resulting output of each pit. Material taken from a segment earns a net benefit per unit that can be positive or negative, and value earned in later periods is discounted relative to earlier periods. The objective is to maximize the total discounted net income over the whole horizon.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the set of pits that can be mined

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the set of extraction segments within a pit, listed in the fixed order in which they must be mined; the first member is the topmost segment and each later member lies deeper and can only be reached after the ones above it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the set of extraction periods in chronological order; the first member is the opening period and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: nev
params.doc: the net extraction benefit earned per unit of material taken from a segment of a pit, in value per unit; it may be negative for segments whose material costs more to handle than it returns

params.name: evo
params.doc: the total volume of material contained in a segment of a pit, in units; this is the most that can ever be taken from that segment across the whole horizon

params.name: demand
params.doc: the product demand that must be met in each period, in units

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to value earned in each period, a dimensionless multiplier that weights earlier periods more than later ones

vars.name: b
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is available to be extracted in a period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment is the last (deepest) segment whose extraction sequence ends in a period for that pit, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: open
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is ever activated for extraction over the whole horizon, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: ej
vars.doc: the position in the segment ordering of the last segment whose sequence ends in a period for a pit; bounded between 0 and the number of segments

vars.name: out
vars.doc: the amount of material extracted from a segment of a pit in a period, in units

vars.name: pout
vars.doc: the total material output of a pit in a period, in units; bounded above by eighty percent of that period's demand

vars.name: obj_var
vars.doc: the total discounted net income earned over the whole horizon, in value

description: In every period, each pit's extraction sequence must end at exactly one of its segments. For each pit and period, exactly one segment is marked as the one where the extraction sequence ends.

description: Within each pit the extraction can only progress downward over time and never move back up. For each pit, the ending position reached in a period must be at least as deep as the ending position reached in the period before it.
expected_pyomo:
def ethree_rule(m, p, t):
    t_list = list(m.t)
    i = t_list.index(t)
    if i == len(t_list) - 1:
        return Constraint.Skip
    return m.ej[p, t] <= m.ej[p, t_list[i + 1]]
model.ethree = Constraint(model.p, model.t, rule=ethree_rule)

description: A segment of a pit becomes available to extract in a period according to how the extraction has unfolded above it and before it. For each pit, segment, and period, availability comes from the segment directly above it having been available but not yet where the sequence ended in that same period, plus that same segment having been where the sequence ended in the previous period. The topmost segment in the opening period is available from the start.

description: A segment must be completely mined out before the segment directly below it in the same pit can be activated. For each pit and segment, the material taken from that segment across all periods must reach its full volume whenever the next deeper segment is activated. The deepest segment has nothing below it and so carries no such requirement.

model_narrative: We plan how to mine a group of open pits over a sequence of time periods. Each pit is divided into segments stacked from top to bottom, and the segments of a pit have to be taken in order from the top down. In every period we decide which segments are available to extract, how far down the extraction has progressed in each pit, how much material to take from each segment, and the resulting output of each pit. Material taken from a segment earns a net benefit per unit that can be positive or negative, and value earned in later periods is discounted relative to earlier periods. The objective is to maximize the total discounted net income over the whole horizon.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the set of pits that can be mined

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the set of extraction segments within a pit, listed in the fixed order in which they must be mined; the first member is the topmost segment and each later member lies deeper and can only be reached after the ones above it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the set of extraction periods in chronological order; the first member is the opening period and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: nev
params.doc: the net extraction benefit earned per unit of material taken from a segment of a pit, in value per unit; it may be negative for segments whose material costs more to handle than it returns

params.name: evo
params.doc: the total volume of material contained in a segment of a pit, in units; this is the most that can ever be taken from that segment across the whole horizon

params.name: demand
params.doc: the product demand that must be met in each period, in units

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to value earned in each period, a dimensionless multiplier that weights earlier periods more than later ones

vars.name: b
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is available to be extracted in a period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment is the last (deepest) segment whose extraction sequence ends in a period for that pit, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: open
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is ever activated for extraction over the whole horizon, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: ej
vars.doc: the position in the segment ordering of the last segment whose sequence ends in a period for a pit; bounded between 0 and the number of segments

vars.name: out
vars.doc: the amount of material extracted from a segment of a pit in a period, in units

vars.name: pout
vars.doc: the total material output of a pit in a period, in units; bounded above by eighty percent of that period's demand

vars.name: obj_var
vars.doc: the total discounted net income earned over the whole horizon, in value

description: The total discounted net income accumulates the value of every bit of material extracted, weighted by its per-unit benefit and discounted by the period in which it is taken. Set the total income equal to the sum over all pits, segments, and periods of the discount factor times the per-unit net benefit times the amount extracted.

vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is available to be extracted in a period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment is the last (deepest) segment whose extraction sequence ends in a period for that pit, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: open

vars.name: ej
vars.doc: the position in the segment ordering of the last segment whose sequence ends in a period for a pit; bounded between 0 and the number of segments

vars.name: out
vars.doc: the amount of material extracted from a segment of a pit in a period, in units

vars.doc: the total material output of a pit in a period, in units; bounded above by eighty percent of that period's demand

vars.name: obj_var
vars.doc: the total discounted net income earned over the whole horizon, in value

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, the extraction sequence of every pit ends in exactly one segment in each period. Second, link the ending position of each pit to which segment its sequence ends at. Third, the ending depth of each pit can only move deeper as time goes on. Fourth, a segment becomes available in a period only by following its predecessor and the prior period. Fifth, each pit's total output in a period is the sum of what is taken from its segments. Sixth, the combined output of all pits in a period must meet that period's demand. Seventh, a segment must be activated whenever it is available to extract in any period. Eighth, a segment is only activated if it is available in at least one period. Ninth, material can be taken from a segment in a period only when it is available, up to its volume. Tenth, a segment must be fully mined out before the segment below it can be activated. Eleventh, the total taken from a segment over the horizon cannot exceed its volume and is zero unless activated. Finally, total discounted net income is the sum over all extractions of discounted per-unit benefit.
